channel.title: Global Sanctions News (Custom RSS)
channel.description: Auto-generated feed from rendered Global Sanctions news list

item.title: EU General Court rejects Beloglazov’s Russia de-listing case & interprets “facilitating sanctions circumvention”
item.description: Beloglazov v Council (T-492/24). The EU General Court has rejected Dimitry Beloglazov’s application to annul his designation under Article 3(1)(h)(i) of Regulation (EU) 269/2014 for facilitating the c

item.title: Dutch bank fined for inadequate sanctions and anti-money laundering controls
item.description: De Nederlandsche Bank has imposed an administrative fine of €8.5 million on ABN AMRO Bank operating under deficient anti-money laundering and sanctions controls between September 2023 and 2024. ABN

item.title: European Public Prosecutor’s Office requests investigation into sanctions circumvention in Poland
item.description: The European Public Prosecutor’s Office (EPPO) has requested law enforcement authorities in Poland to investigate a case of suspected circumvention of Russia sanctions. The suspect is suspected of hav

item.title: EU statement on third country alignment with chemical weapons designations
item.description: The EU has issued a statement on third country alignment with EU designations against the proliferation and use of chemical weapons. On 3 July 2026, 6 people said to be linked to the death of Alexei N

item.title: US updates Hong Kong designations following expiry of national emergency
item.description: The US has delisted people listed under EO 13936 following its expiry. The EO declared a national emergency in Hong Kong in 2020 and was last renewed in July 2025. It expired on 14 July 2026 and has n

item.title: UK amends arbitration costs general licence to introduce new reporting requirement
item.description: The UK has amended General Licence INT/2025/5787748 to introduce a new monthly reporting requirement. The GL allows payments to be made to arbitration associations and arbitrators to cover fees and ex

item.title: EU adds 1 person and 5 entities linked to Russian drone manufacturing
item.description: The EU has designated 1 person and 5 entities following Russia’s strikes on Kyiv on 1 and 5 July 2026 – press release. Those designated are said to be involved in drone manufacturing for Russia’s mili

item.title: US announces new visa restrictions on far-left groups
item.description: The US Department of State has announced a new visa restriction policy on members of far-left groups – press release. The policy will target members of far-left groups (and other aligned groups) that

item.title: US designates 2 Mexican cartels as foreign terrorist organisations
item.description: The US has designated the Juárez Cartel and Los Viagras as Foreign Terrorist Organizations (FTOs) and Specially Designated Global Terrorists (SDGTs) – press release. The groups are narco-terrorist ca

item.title: UN lists Congo River Alliance military group and paramilitary leaders
item.description: The Resolution 1533 (2004) UN Security Council Sanctions Committee has added 6 people and 2 entities to its DRC sanctions list. Those designated are: Alliance Fleuve Congo (a.k.a. the Congo River
